<!--
Thanks for contributing! Keep PRs focused and small.
Title should follow Conventional Commits, e.g. "feat(datasets): add parquet support".
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do and why? -->

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `refactor` — behaviour-preserving change
- [ ] `docs` — documentation
- [ ] `test` — tests
- [ ] `ci` / `build` / `chore` — tooling

## Related issues

<!-- e.g. Closes #123 -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Follows the vertical-slice pattern (model → repo → service → schema → endpoint → tests) where applicable
- [ ] Backend gates pass: `ruff`, `black --check`, `mypy`, `pytest`
- [ ] Frontend gates pass: `typecheck`, `lint`, `test`, `build`
- [ ] Added/updated tests for the change
- [ ] Updated documentation if behaviour changed
- [ ] No secrets, credentials or `.env` files committed
- [ ] Commits follow Conventional Commits and are atomic
